name: Saw of Shamash
description: The Saw of Shamash is a legendary artifact of immense power in Babylonian mythology, associated with the sun god Shamash (also known as Utu). Shamash, the god of justice, law, morality, and truth, wielded this saw as an instrument of divine judgment and cosmic order. It is said to be forged from the celestial metal of the heavens, imbued with the very essence of Shamash's radiant light and unwavering righteousness. The saw was not merely a tool for cutting; it was a symbol of Shamash's ability to dissect truth from falsehood, to separate good from evil, and to enforce divine law upon the mortal realm. Its teeth were sharp enough to cleave through mountains, and its edge shone with the brilliance of the sun. Legends tell that Shamash used the Saw of Shamash to carve pathways through the mountains at dawn and dusk, allowing the sun to rise and set each day. The saw also played a crucial role in dispensing justice, as Shamash would use it to punish the wicked and reward the virtuous. The very sight of the Saw of Shamash instilled fear in the hearts of wrongdoers and inspired hope in the oppressed. Its power extended beyond mere physical cutting; it could sever bonds of deceit, expose hidden truths, and restore balance to the world. Ownership of the Saw of Shamash was believed to grant its wielder incredible power and authority, but only those who possessed a pure heart and a commitment to justice could truly harness its divine potential. The saw represents the ultimate power of divine law and the unwavering pursuit of truth and justice in the Babylonian worldview. Some accounts suggest it could also be used to open portals to other dimensions, specifically the underworld, further solidifying Shamash's role as a mediator between worlds.
subtitle: Divine Instrument of Justice and Truth
origin: Forged in the heavens by the divine smiths for Shamash.
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly hidden in the celestial realm or lost in the underworld.

keyMyths.title: Shamash's Daily Journey
keyMyths.description: Shamash uses the Saw of Shamash to carve his path through the mountains, allowing the sun to rise and set each day, maintaining cosmic order. This ensures the continuation of life and the cycles of nature.

keyMyths.title: Judgment of the Wicked
keyMyths.description: Shamash employs the Saw of Shamash to punish the wicked and uphold justice, ensuring that those who violate divine law face retribution. This reinforces the importance of morality and ethical conduct.

sources.author: Stephanie Dalley
sources.description: Collection of translated Mesopotamian myths and legends.
sources.text: Collection of translated Mesopotamian myths and legends.

sources.author: Henri Frankfort
sources.description: Analysis of Mesopotamian religion and its connection to kingship.
